baseline.note: Oracle-achievable ceiling: a perfect reviewer lands every held-out case inside its gold band with a valid {rating, notes} object. Re-baseline after Phase 4.5 if a live framework's best stabilises below 1.0 on the swapped-in interview labels.

output.notes: off-by-one on [1,2,3]; change items[len(items)] to items[-1]

band_cases.desc: Correct: guards the empty list and returns the final element for every list; no logic bug.
Off-by-one: indexes one past the end and fails for every non-empty list; fix to items[len(items) - 1] or items[-1].

structure_assertions.assert: A perfect-oracle generate (gold-band midpoint + non-empty note) scores 1.0 over all band cases.
An empty emission ({}) scores 0.0 on every band case (no saturation trap).
A non-{rating, notes} object scores 0.0: extra key, bool/float/string rating, out-of-range rating, or empty notes all fail the strict schema.
The two seed bands are disjoint ([85,100] and [0,45]), so a constant-rating agent scores at most 0.5 — the metric cannot be faked by emitting one fixed number.
The labeled set exercises both ends of the lens: a correct empty-guarded accessor (LE-001, [85,100]) and an off-by-one that is wrong for every non-empty input (LE-002, [0,45]).
